text.title: Knowledge Sharing Ritual Set Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the Knowledge Sharing Ritual Set methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every ritual instance MUST produce at least one persisted artefact (notes, recording, ADR, action-items list); rituals that produce nothing are auto-cancelled after 2 misses.
rule.rationale: rituals without artefact decay into social meetings within 6 weeks.

rule.statement: Cadence is fixed: weekly tech-talk 60min, biweekly demo 45min, monthly architecture review 90min, quarterly skip-level 45min/person; variations require an explicit ADR.
rule.rationale: drifting cadence kills the muscle memory; calendar-stable beats topic-stable.

rule.statement: Each ritual has a named presenter on the calendar invite 7 days ahead; un-staffed slots are explicit holes flagged in the artefact, not silent no-shows.
rule.rationale: without a named presenter the ritual becomes ad-hoc and quality collapses.

rule.statement: Skip-level notes MUST aggregate themes, never quote a named individual without consent; quotes require explicit opt-in.
rule.rationale: named quotes destroy the safety that makes skip-level useful.

rule.statement: Action items emitted by any ritual MUST have (owner, due_date); un-assigned items are forbidden and surfaced as ritual debt.
rule.rationale: open-ended action items pile up and credibility of the ritual erodes.
